job: Turn one creator capsule into a checked Remotion composition contract and generated React/Remotion stub so a creator can move from memory card to timeline-based video without adding Remotion to the DASH core or committing rendered media.

remotion_adapter.runtime_boundary: Remotion, React rendering, codecs, and final export live in an external creator project. DASH emits only a checked JSON contract, fixed-canvas proof card, and ignored .artifacts TSX handoff so the core stays small and no rendered video enters git.
dependencies: external Remotion and React install in the creator workspace
DASH core keeps only JSON, HTML proof, and the Bun validation script
rendered video files stay outside git unless separately reviewed as tiny public previews

sequences.title: Start with the capsule
sequences.visual: A compact memory card enters on a dark editorial stage and locks intent, creator, surface, and proof into view.
sequences.animation: Card slides up over twelve frames, the proof stamp fades in, and a signal line traces the capsule edge.
sequences.voiceover_intent: Tell the creator this composition starts from stored intent, not from a blank timeline.

sequences.title: Memory survives the cut
sequences.visual: Four capsule fields split into timeline lanes: intent, grammar, blocked inputs, and remix handles.
sequences.animation: Lanes stagger in by six frames, then settle into a clean editorial rhythm with one accent pulse.
sequences.voiceover_intent: Show which parts of the creative brief must survive every future edit.

sequences.title: Timeline becomes explicit
sequences.visual: The Remotion sequence rail appears with six labeled beats and deterministic frame numbers.
sequences.animation: A playhead sweeps from frame 0 to 180 while each beat lights only inside its own frame range.
sequences.voiceover_intent: Make the handoff feel executable by naming exact sequence starts, durations, and frame probes.

sequences.title: Style tokens, not screenshots
sequences.visual: Palette chips, type scale notes, and motion rules replace any imported media or account screenshot.
sequences.animation: Chips snap to a baseline grid and a warning chip rejects raw image, audio, video, and private paths.
sequences.voiceover_intent: Normalize public-safe visual grammar instead of copying private assets into a video project.

sequences.title: Proof before render
sequences.visual: A gate lists creator:remotion-scene-check, browser measure, print proof, security scan, and CI before export.
sequences.animation: Each gate item checks on; the final render command stays dim until all proof notes are visible.
sequences.voiceover_intent: Make the creator wait for proof before opening the external renderer or exporting video.

sequences.title: External renderer handoff
sequences.visual: The generated TSX stub ticket, composition id, and render command sit beside remix handles for the next creator pass.
sequences.animation: Ticket stamps external runtime, remix handles orbit once, and the timeline fades to a copyable handoff card.
sequences.voiceover_intent: Leave the creator with a real Remotion stub and a safe next step without bloating DASH.

blocked_moves: adding Remotion or React as a DASH core dependency from a single adapter win
committing rendered MP4 output, codec caches, or raw source media
using private prompts, client copy, provider logs, account screenshots, cookies, or secrets
skipping browser measure and print proof for the fixed-canvas proof card

blocked: private prompts or private client copy
raw generated image, video, audio, rendered exports, and renderer caches
local absolute paths and provider execution logs
API keys, cookies, tokens, secrets, and account screenshots
unreviewed third-party assets or proprietary fonts

retention: Retain creator-remotion-scene-route as an edge adapter: JSON contract, generated ignored TSX composition stub, fixed-canvas proof card, and validation script. This is the second capsule-to-code renderer handoff after p5, but still not enough to promote a shared package primitive.
